body.title: Agent + capability-mixins-over-Domains, and retire Discworld class names for functional ones
body.text:
STATUS: decision-in-progress, CAPTURED FOR REVIEW. Deliberately spawns NO tickets yet — this is the largest refactor to date and must go through the interview/reconcile step before it becomes work (the architecture's own principle: intentions reconcile before they compile). Forward extension of [[D-domain-object-encapsulation-2026-07-01]] and the thesis note architecture/compiled-inference-thesis (stance-vs-capability = judgment-vs-resolver layer).

WHY (Akien, 2026-07-02): the Discworld class names have become illegible — 'it's unclear who does what.' Separately, the just-shipped D-domain-object-encapsulation made CodingDomain a standalone object (owns selection/prompts/loop/escalation) that DS merely delegates to. Those two threads converge into one refactor that is BIG but SIMPLIFYING: one Agent + composable capability-mixins replaces a pile of bespoke, character-named device classes.

THE SHAPE:
- `Agent(~)` — the root. Lifecycle, bus, identity, the glue. Everything is an Agent.
- CAPABILITY = a MIXIN that wraps a Domain object. `CodingCapability` HAS-A `CodingDomain` (composition, NOT identity — the Domain already stands alone as the reusable unit; a non-agent caller can use CodingDomain directly, and making the mixin the domain would re-fuse what D-domain-object-encapsulation just separated). The mixin is thin glue that exposes the domain's capability on an Agent by delegating to it (exactly as DS._run_inference now delegates to resolve_domain('coding').run()).
- A concrete agent COMPOSES: `CodeBuilder = Agent + CodingCapability + glue`. Instance `DS.0` (handle TBD) is an instance of CodeBuilder.
- GENERALIZATION: 'coding' generalizes to other task types the same way Domain already generalizes — BaseDomain -> CodingDomain today, -> ResearchDomain / ReviewDomain / etc. later. Add a domain + its capability mixin; you do NOT subclass a new bespoke agent. Homogeneity over special-case: one mechanism (capability-mixin over Domain), parameterized by domain.
- THREE LAYERS become KINDS OF MIXIN, not a rigid inheritance spine (this is the improvement over the first-draft Resolver/Judge/Governor base-class idea): capability mixins wrap starving Domains (resolver layer); governance/judgment concerns are their own mixins. An agent that is both a builder AND holds an admin concern simply carries both mixins. Mixins compose where a spine cannot.
- NAMING: retire Discworld CLASS names for functional ones (CodeBuilder, Administrator, Interviewer, ...). CRITICAL CONSTRAINT — the character names were silently encoding stance-vs-capability (judgment-never-starves vs resolver-starves-to-zero); the rename must PRESERVE that signal by making layer/role explicit (via mixin category / role), so 'who does what' AND 'what compiles away' both get MORE legible, not less. Classes-vs-instances holds: functional class = the kind, `.0` = the instance, `to=` addresses the instance (consistent with the Granny/GrannyWeatherwax rule).

ROLE COROLLARY (from thread 2, same session): Vetinari is a STRICT administrator and the WRONG device to interview anyone ('would you want him interviewing you?'); the intention-interview needs a people-person — Moist von Lipwig (people-reader, con-man-turned-public-servant). Admin (Vetinari/Administrator) != design/intake (Moist/Interviewer). Under the new naming these become functional roles/mixins too. (Moist/Lipwig does not exist as a device yet.)

OPEN QUESTIONS to reconcile before spawning tickets:
- Q1: compose-vs-is-a for the capability mixin (recommendation: HAS-A / compose; confirm).
- Q2: do instance HANDLES also de-Discworld (DS.0 -> CodeBuilder.0 / cb.0) or keep terse handles as pure addresses (less churn in dispatch/availability-flags/tmux-session names)?
- Q3: the exact layer/role taxonomy and how it is expressed (mixin category? role tag? marker base?) so the stance/capability signal survives the rename.
- Q4: migration order + blast radius — every device inherits BaseDevice/BaseShim today; how Agent relates to BaseDevice (is Agent the new BaseDevice, or a layer above?).

NEXT: day-close 2026-07-02, restart fresh on a new day to tackle this as its own effort (a large refactor deserves an uncluttered session — context-rot is the enemy here).
